channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How America Quietly Made Nuclear Power Great Again
item.description: The benefits of an expedited nuclear reactor approval process are coming closer to fruition.

item.title: OpenAI didn't contact police despite employees flagging mass shooter's concerning chatbot interactions: REPORT
item.description: Months before a mass school shooting in British Columbia, OpenAI employees raised alarm about the shooter's violent chatbot prompts and debated callin...

item.title: If the recent AI and crypto shocks upset you, you’re tracking the wrong cycle
item.description: Claude and DeepSeek, separated at birth?

item.title: Georgian Gorgeous, Courtesy of Gainsborough and the Frick
item.description: Couture with dash and sparkle delivered in New York’s first Gainsborough show.

item.title: The CEO of a $1 billion AI unicorn says his peers in Silicon Valley want you to fear for your job, but they’re actually first on the chopping block
item.description: Tanmai Gopal of PromptQL said tech people have a bad habit of thinking "this affects me. So it's going to affect everyone like that."

item.title: ICE is quietly buying warehouses for detention centers and leaving local officials out of the loop
item.description: In a Texas town at the edge of the Rio Grande and a tall metal border wall, rumors swirled that federal immigration officials wanted to purchase three...

item.title: DOGE’s Medicaid data dump aims to expose fraud — but privacy and legal hurdles loom
item.description: Elon Musk is touting the Department of Government Efficiency’s release of open-source Medicaid data as a transparency breakthrough. But prosecutors an...

item.title: ‘I have a chip on my shoulder.’ Phoebe Gates wants her $185 million AI startup Phia to succeed with ‘no ties to my privilege or my last name’
item.description: The daughter of Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates acknowledges her privilege, but says she’s building her own thing without their help.

item.title: How fandom became culture’s power center — and a blueprint for Gen Z’s economic influence
item.description: Fandoms sit at the intersection of identity, influence, and spending. Three dynamics explain their accelerating economic power.

item.title: Peter Thiel and other tech billionaires are publicly shielding their children from the products that made them rich
item.description: As governments race to restrict minors’ social media use, the tech billionaires who built the platforms are imposing strict screen limits at home.

item.title: Sheriffs plot ICE cooperation 'workarounds' after new Maryland law bans cooperation with immigration officers
item.description: Maryland sheriff fears ending law enforcement's cooperation with ICE will lead to more chaos on the streets, after Maryland Gov. Wes Moore signed legi...

item.title: Labor Dept deploys ‘strike team’ to California over $21B unemployment debt, fraud concerns
item.description: Labor Department deploys specialized "strike team" to California over alleged unemployment fraud and $21 billion in improper payments during the pande...

item.title: Food of the Future? I’ll Pass
item.description: Meat simulations won’t convince our tastebuds.

item.title: Justice Department fires interim U.S. attorney hours after appointment
item.description: Almost immediately after James Hundley was appointed interim U.S. attorney of the Eastern District of Virginia to replace the predecessor who was foun...

item.title: Trump approves DC emergency declaration over Potomac sewage spill, FEMA mobilizes
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a disaster declaration for Washington, D.C., allowing FEMA to coordinate emergency relief efforts over the Potomac River...

item.title: CIA Yanks 19 Docs ‘Compromised’ By Leftist Activism, Including Threat Assessment Targeting ‘Traditional Motherhood’
item.description: The CIA’s commitment to advancing leftist activism appears to span at least three presidential administrations beginning in 2015.

item.title: Judge gives reprieve to Trump after slavery exhibits already restored to Philadelphia park
item.description: A federal appeals court judge delivered a brief reprieve to the Trump administration Friday in its battle to take down slavery exhibits at the Preside...

item.title: All truckers and bus drivers will be required to take commercial driver's license tests in English
item.description: All truckers and bus drivers will have to take their commercial driver's license tests in English as the Trump administration expands its aggressive c...

item.title: America Slides Into A Congress-Shaped Hole, And Gorsuch Helps
item.description: Our discussion, in media and in law, has been about Trump's excesses and boundary-crossing. A better starting place would be the laziness and uselessn...

item.title: Liu > Gu
item.description: The American figure skater’s performance off the ice stands in stark contrast with that of the California-born, China-embracing skier.

item.title: Courts Won’t Stop VA Dems’ Gerrymandering Gambit, So Where Is The GOP’s Ground Game?
item.description: Republicans cannot use lawsuits as their only strategy to stop gerrymandering. They need be in campaign mode, knocking doors, and getting people to th...

item.title: 4 Of The Best Lines From Kavanaugh’s Masterclass Defense Of Trump’s Tariff Power
item.description: 'As they interpret the statute, the President could, for example, block all imports from China but cannot order even a $1 tariff on goods imported fro...

item.title: Texas congressman under ethics investigation over alleged affair with staffer who killed herself
item.description: A congressional ethics watchdog is investigating Texas GOP Rep. Tony Gonzales over his reported affair with a staffer who later died after setting her...

item.title: The Supreme Court Keeps the Taxing Power with Congress
item.description: The Court got the tariff case right. Trump should accept that and move on — and he may end up thanking the justices.

item.title: Kavanaugh: Trump Obviously Has Tariff Power And Here’s How He Can Keep Using It
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court may have ruled against President Trump’s use of an emergency economics law to impose tariffs on foreign countries, but that doe...

item.title: Why Static Portfolios Fail When Risk Regimes Change
item.description: Why static portfolio frameworks fail when risk regimes shift, drawing lessons from the very different market breakdowns of 2020 and 2022. The post Why...

item.title: What the Market Knows That WACC Doesn’t
item.description: Market-implied discount rates reveal how investors price risk, often diverging from WACC and reshaping capital decisions. The post What the Market Kno...

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...
